summary: RhinoPlanner feature tested - backend API fully working, frontend UI partially working. Canvas drawing and all controls function but SVG anatomical background diagrams not loading.

ui_bugs.issue: SVG anatomical nose diagrams (frontal, profile, base views) not loading on Fabric.js canvas. Canvas appears blank/white. FabricImage.fromURL silently fails to load the data:image/svg+xml base64 encoded SVG backgrounds. Drawing still works but without the anatomical reference diagrams.

action_items: FIX: Canvas SVG background images not loading in RhinoPlannerPage.jsx - FabricImage.fromURL fails to load data:image/svg+xml;base64 encoded SVGs for the anatomical nose diagrams. Drawing works but without reference diagrams.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (8/8 E2E tests passing, but canvas SVG background bug present)

seed_data_creation: Used existing test patient ID: e1778a07-50f5-41d0-8c2e-5675ae5b6a63
context_for_next_testing_agent: RhinoPlanner APIs fully working. Frontend canvas drawing works but SVG nose diagram backgrounds not rendering. All E2E tests pass (page loads, navigation, save, notes input, procedure checkboxes). Main issue is visual - the anatomical reference images are missing from the canvas.
rca_of_issue: Canvas SVG background loading issue - The createNoseDiagramFrontal(), createNoseDiagramProfile(), createNoseDiagramBase() functions create data:image/svg+xml;base64 encoded SVGs. FabricImage.fromURL is called in initCanvas() but silently fails. Possible causes: 1) Fabric.js v7 API change for loading data URLs, 2) Base64 encoding issue with Polish characters in SVG labels, 3) Canvas not ready when background load is attempted. Suggested fix: Try using FabricImage.fromURL with different options, or load SVG as raw img element first then convert.
